target_game.name: Roof Rails
target_game.core_loop: Run forward, collect pole pieces to extend your balancing pole, use the pole to slide on rails between rooftops, and avoid obstacles that cut the pole.
target_game.audience_proxy: Broad casual audience, appealing to all ages and genders looking for quick, accessible entertainment.
target_game.visual_style: 3D minimalist with simple geometry, bright pastel colors, and a clean, uncluttered aesthetic.

palette.description: Bright cyan and blue cityscape with a stark white path and vibrant yellow and magenta accents.

top_archetypes.label: Satisfaction · in-game

centroid_hook.summary: Rapid-fire block placement clearing a full grid.
centroid_hook.visual_action: Green blocks are slotted into a grid at high speed until the board is full and clears.

top_archetypes.rationale: 1 creatives across 1 unique advertisers, average age 3d (velocity source: sov). Hook representative: "Rapid-fire block placement clearing a full grid."

top_archetypes.label: Curiosity · 3D-render

centroid_hook.summary: A dramatic crime scene investigation at night with police and forensics.
centroid_hook.visual_action: Police tape is stretched across a mailbox while a forensic photographer flashes a camera inside a house.

top_archetypes.rationale: 1 creatives across 1 unique advertisers, average age 5d (velocity source: sov). Hook representative: "A dramatic crime scene investigation at night with police and forensics."

top_archetypes.label: Fail · 3D-render

centroid_hook.summary: A cat jumps on a keyboard, causing a man to fall over.
centroid_hook.visual_action: An orange cat leaps onto a computer desk, startling a man who falls backward in his chair.

top_archetypes.rationale: 1 creatives across 1 unique advertisers, average age 381d (velocity source: sov). Hook representative: "A cat jumps on a keyboard, causing a man to fall over."

top_archetypes.label: Curiosity · animation

centroid_hook.summary: Cross-section reveal of a secret underground bunker beneath the Eiffel Tower.
centroid_hook.visual_action: A detailed 2D illustration of a multi-level bunker is shown beneath a landmark, transitioning to other locations.

top_archetypes.rationale: 3 creatives across 3 unique advertisers, average age 240d (velocity source: sov). Hook representative: "Cross-section reveal of a secret underground bunker beneath the Eiffel Tower."

top_archetypes.label: Satisfaction · mixed

centroid_hook.summary: A hand clears colorful buses from a crowded lot while a voiceover asks about relaxation.
centroid_hook.visual_action: A hand with a stylus moves buses out of a grid to clear a path.
centroid_hook.text_overlay: Hey! Played a game that Actually makes you Feel relaxed?
centroid_hook.voiceover_transcript: Hey! Have you ever played a game that actually makes you feel relaxed?

top_archetypes.rationale: 3 creatives across 3 unique advertisers, average age 81d (velocity source: sov). Hook representative: "A hand clears colorful buses from a crowded lot while a voiceover asks about rel"

game_fit_scores.rationale: Audience overlap is real (broad hypercasual satisfaction-seekers), but mechanically this is a block-placement/grid-fill puzzle hook with zero relationship to Roof Rails' running, pole-extending, rail-sliding loop — recreating it would require fabricating gameplay that doesn't exist in the app, risking misleading-ad flags. Palette (green/indigo/yellow) also clashes with the cyan/pastel cityscape DNA. Skip a direct copy; if the team wants to chase the "satisfaction" emotional pitch, adapt it natively via rapid pole-piece pickup montages or rail-slide combo clears with a "GENIUS/PSYCHOPATH" tier overlay.

Severe mismatch: the dark crime-scene aesthetic (#1A1A2E/gold/brown, night, forensic tone) directly contradicts Roof Rails' bright pastel cyan minimalist look, and there is no plausible bridge from "police tape and forensics" into a pole-balancing rooftop runner — any transition would feel like a bait-and-switch and risk store policy flags. Audience skews toward narrative/mystery/casual-puzzle viewers rather than pick-up-and-play hypercasual runners, and the cluster is tiny (1 creative, 1 advertiser) so there is no validated demand signal. Recommend skip; do not greenlight a Roof Rails adaptation of this archetype.

This is a Gardenscapes-style live-action/3D fail hook with a cat-and-pratfall gag — it has zero mechanical overlap with Roof Rails' pole-balancing rooftop runner loop, and showing a desk/office scene wastes the chance to demo collectible pole pieces and saw obstacles. Palette (greens/oranges) and indoor setting also clash with the bright cyan minimalist cityscape DNA. Only the broad casual audience modestly overlaps; recommend skipping unless reframed as a 3D-rendered "fail-then-win" gag where the character's pole gets cut short — otherwise irrelevant for this title.

Major friction across the board: the archetype's dark, earthy palette (navy/brown/olive) and detailed 2D illustrated cross-section style is the polar opposite of Roof Rails' bright pastel 3D minimalism, and a static landmark reveal communicates none of the run/collect/slide loop that drives install intent for this title. Audience overlap is only mildly plausible since curiosity hooks travel well in broad casual, but without a credible narrative bridge to pole-balancing rooftop runs the concept will mislead users and tank retention. Recommend skipping unless the team can prototype a hybrid where the cross-section reveals a stacked-rooftop course — otherwise reallocate budget to gameplay-forward or satisfying-fail archetypes.

The "relaxing bus jam" satisfaction hook is a puzzle/sort archetype that fundamentally misrepresents Roof Rails' fast-paced runner core loop — there are no rails, pole-balancing, or forward motion in the hook, so installs would be heavily mismatched and D1 retention would suffer. Palette is adjacent-casual but the coral/teal mix clashes with the game's signature cyan-and-yellow rooftop aesthetic, and the "relaxed" emotional pitch directly contradicts the game's energetic/competitive UI mood. Audience overlap (broad casual, all ages/genders) is the only real bridge; recommend skipping unless we test a hybrid where the bus-clearing hook hard-cuts at 3s into authentic rail-sliding gameplay.

brief.title: GENIUS → PSYCHOPATH Pole Combo
brief.hook_3s: Pole explodes from 3 to 30 pieces in a cyan blur as the runner rockets onto a glowing rail — tier label snaps from SMART to PSYCHOPATH.
scene_flow: 0–2s: Tight over-shoulder on the runner sprinting down the white rooftop path; first burst of yellow pole pieces vacuums into the pole — overlay flashes 'SMART'.
2–6s: Rapid-fire pickup montage — pole extends frame-by-frame as dozens of pieces snap on in rhythm; counter ticks 8 → 17 → 26. Overlay flashes 'GENIUS'.
6–10s: Runner launches onto a long cyan rail spanning two skyscrapers, pole balancing perfectly while saw obstacles shave off pieces in satisfying chunks but the pole survives. Overlay: 'GENIUS+'.
10–13s: Mega-clear moment — pole slams into a wall of diamonds, screen pops with yellow/magenta confetti, 'PSYCHOPATH' overlay slams in.
13–15s: Cut to level-complete screen with diamond payout and progress bar filling; CTA card 'PLAY FREE' over the cyan skyline.
brief.visual_direction: Lean fully into Roof Rails' 3D minimalist DNA: stark white rooftop runway, bright cyan (#5CD6FF) sky and rails, pastel cyan (#EAF6FA) building blocks, vibrant yellow (#FFC824) pole pieces and diamond bursts. Camera stays at the signature Roof Rails over-shoulder/low-chase angle. Cuts are punchy and on-beat to sell the rapid-fire satisfaction pitch borrowed from the block-clear archetype, but every visual is native gameplay — no fabricated mechanics. Tier overlays use bold sans-serif white type with a yellow flash, slamming in on impact frames.
brief.rationale: Voodoo's current Roof Rails rotation is all unlabeled video + a playable with no copy hook — they own the mechanic but ship zero emotional/tier-label framing. This brief imports the trending 'SMART → GENIUS → PSYCHOPATH' satisfaction-tier overlay (the winning archetype) and grafts it onto authentic pole-pickup and rail-slide footage, giving UA a fresh thumb-stopping text hook without fabricating gameplay. Test against the existing copy-less videos as the control; expect lift on IPM from the tier-escalation curiosity loop.
scenario_prompts: Hero frame, aspect 9:16, authentic Roof Rails in-game screenshot: low over-the-shoulder chase camera on a faceless minimalist runner sprinting down a stark white rooftop path, balancing an extremely long horizontal yellow (#FFC824) pole studded with collectible pole-piece segments, mid-slide entering a glowing cyan (#5CD6FF) rail that stretches between two pastel cyan (#EAF6FA) skyscraper blocks, tiny diamond currency icons floating along the path, top-left progress bar and level indicator HUD, bold white sans-serif overlay text 'PSYCHOPATH' slamming center-frame with a yellow flash, 3D minimalist with simple geometry, bright pastel colors, and a clean, uncluttered aesthetic, bright cyan sky, no clutter, no fabricated UI

brief.title: "Ever Played Something This Satisfying?" — Roof Rails Pole-Stack ASMR
brief.hook_3s: A whispered "Have you ever played a game THIS satisfying?" as a giant pole snaps onto the runner mid-stride, then carves a long shimmering slide across a cyan rooftop rail.
scene_flow: 0–3s HOOK: First-person-ish over-shoulder view; voiceover asks 'Ever played a game that actually feels THIS satisfying?' as the character grabs a cluster of yellow pole pieces — pole telescopes outward with a crisp click-click-click SFX.
3–7s ESCALATION: Character mounts a long white rail between two pastel skyscrapers; pole balances horizontally, sparks of cyan trail behind. Diamond counter ticks up rapidly. Overlay: 'wait for it…'
7–11s PAYOFF: A saw obstacle clips the pole shorter — gasp moment — then a pickup pile re-extends it to absurd length, sliding across THREE rails in one combo. Big yellow '+250' burst.
11–14s VARIETY: Quick montage of three rooftop sections (sunset pastel, cyan noon, magenta accent) all cleared in one unbroken slide — emphasizes the 'relaxing flow state' promise.
14–15s CTA: Game logo snaps in over a final perfect landing; tap-to-play finger pulse on screen.
brief.visual_direction: Lean hard into Roof Rails' signature 3D minimalist look: stark white rooftop path, #5CD6FF cyan sky and building blocks, #EAF6FA off-white surfaces, #FFC824 yellow pole pieces and diamond pickups as the visual heroes. Camera stays in the authentic chase-cam runner POV — never break to a hand/stylus puzzle frame (that's the source archetype's mistake). Add subtle ASMR-style polish: slight motion blur on slides, satisfying particle pops on each pole-piece pickup, soft shadow under the runner. UI chrome (top progress bar, diamond counter top-right, level indicator) stays exactly as in-game so the ad reads as authentic gameplay, not a render.
text_overlays: Ever played a game THIS satisfying?
brief.rationale: Voodoo's current Roof Rails rotation is 100% wordless gameplay videos plus one playable — they have ZERO voiceover-led "satisfaction question" hooks, which is the fastest-growing archetype in the casual scan (3 advertisers, 81d avg age). This brief imports the proven VO question-hook and ASMR pacing but anchors it to authentic rail-sliding footage, fixing the puzzle/runner mismatch flagged in game-fit. UA should test against the existing silent-gameplay videos on Applovin first; expect higher 3s hold-rate from the VO question and similar IPM from preserved core-loop fidelity.

brief.title: Pole Too Short?! — Fail-to-Win Rooftop Run
brief.hook_3s: A spinning saw slices the pole down to a stub mid-slide — the runner wobbles over a cyan skyline canyon as the screen freezes on "OH NO!"
scene_flow: 0-3s: Cold open mid-run on a white rooftop rail; a buzzing yellow saw swings in and SHEARS the pole down to a tiny stub. Freeze-frame wobble, comedic 'OH NO' overlay.
3-7s: 'Fail' replay — character teeters, slow-mo drop between two pastel skyscrapers. Big red 'DON'T DO THIS' stamp.
7-11s: Rewind whoosh. This time a finger-tap drags the runner through a stream of glowing pole pieces; the pole grows comically long, magenta sparkles trailing.
11-14s: Triumphant slide across THREE connected rails, ducking saws, diamonds vacuumed up, level bar fills to MAX with a satisfying chime.
14-15s: Logo lockup on cyan sky, CTA pulses.
brief.visual_direction: Lock to Roof Rails' signature 3D minimalist look: chunky low-poly rooftops, stark #EAF6FA white paths, #5CD6FF cyan sky gradient, #FFC824 yellow accents on pole pieces and saws, magenta diamond pickups. Soft global lighting, no textures — just clean flat shading. Camera is the standard behind-the-shoulder Roof Rails follow-cam with a slight Dutch tilt on the fail beat for comedy. Keep HUD authentic: top-left diamond counter, top-center level progress bar, level indicator pill. Saw obstacles are flat yellow discs with triangular teeth, exactly as in-game.
brief.rationale: Voodoo's current Roof Rails rotation is almost entirely no-copy gameplay videos and a single playable — there is zero "fail-first / wrong-way vs right-way" comedic framing in market, even though Fail · 3D-render is a proven scroll-stopper across casual UA. This brief imports that emotional pitch but anchors it to Roof Rails' actual saw-cuts-pole moment, so the hook is on-mechanic (no Gardenscapes cat detour) and the "DON'T DO THIS → DO THIS" structure doubles as a tutorial, lifting IPM for low-intent broad-casual traffic.
